book_claims.claim: Part of a nutritionally complete daily diet
book_claims.mechanism: Provides protein, healthy fats, fibre and minerals as part of a plant-based diet

book_claims.claim: Six-month RCT showed eating cholesterol-lowering foods including almonds led to 15% reduction in blood cholesterol
book_claims.mechanism: Rich in vitamin E (antioxidant), monounsaturated fats, soluble fibre that reduces intestinal cholesterol absorption

supplementary_research.finding: Meta-analysis (Lee-Bravatti et al., 2019) of 15 RCTs found almond consumption significantly reduced total cholesterol and LDL cholesterol while preserving HDL, with effects strongest at doses of 45g/day or more.

RCT (Dikariyanto et al., 2021) found replacing typical snacks with 56g almonds/day for 6 weeks improved endothelial function and reduced LDL cholesterol in adults with above-average cardiovascular risk.

Systematic review found almond consumption improved gut microbiome composition, increasing Bifidobacterium and Lactobacillus populations while the prebiotic fibre and polyphenols in almond skins serve as substrates for beneficial bacteria.

culinary_pairings.source: Vitamin E + anthocyanins for combined antioxidant protection
Almond + oat porridge; complementary fibres and fats
Vitamin C enhances vitamin E regeneration; Sicilian almond-citrus tradition

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Almonds are the richest common nut source of alpha-tocopherol. 30g serving provides 37% RDA. Fat-soluble; absorbed with the almond's own monounsaturated fats.
30g provides 20% RDA. Soaking reduces phytic acid and improves absorption. Critical for 300+ enzyme reactions including energy metabolism.
Same heart-protective oleic acid as olive oil. Reduces LDL without lowering HDL cholesterol.
